pi_items.source: Code verification: setup_task.sh lines 42-43 show these exact names are created as duplicate Company objects in the database for testing purposes

Code verification: setup_task.sh line 41 creates canonical Company with full_name="Yuneec International". Web search confirms Yuneec International is the real company: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yuneec_International
Code verification: task_utils.sh line 9 defines AEROBRIDGE_ADMIN_URL="http://localhost:8000/admin/". This is also used in setup_task.sh line 110

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a local Aerobridge Django application running at http://localhost:8000/admin/. The database contains three Yuneec manufacturer records: the canonical 'Yuneec International' (a real company per Wikipedia), plus two test duplicates 'Yuneec' and 'Yuneec Electric'. Test aircraft include real Yuneec product models: Typhoon H (confirmed real model, launched 2016), Breeze 4K (confirmed real model, now discontinued), and Mantis Q (confirmed real model with voice control). All aircraft initially linked to duplicate manufacturers must be re-linked to the canonical manufacturer, then duplicates deleted without data loss.
